CREATE TABLE test_backup_context (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_backup_context (id, data) VALUES (1, 'test data 1');
INSERT INTO test_backup_context (id, data) VALUES (2, 'test data 2');
SELECT * FROM test_backup_context WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_backup_context WHERE data LIKE 'test%');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, data FROM test_backup_context WHERE data <> 'invalid data') AS valid_data;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_backup_context) AS total_count FROM test_backup_context;
CREATE TABLE additional_context (id INT, context_data VARCHAR(255));
SELECT a.id, b.context_data FROM test_backup_context a JOIN additional_context b ON a.id = b.id;
INSERT INTO test_backup_context (id, data) VALUES (3, 'test data 3');
